howItWorks.intro: This sun exposure & vitamin d calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Vitamin D ≈ Base Rate × (UV Index ÷ 6) × (Skin Exposed % ÷ 25) × Minutes
Base rates vary by skin type (1000 IU/session for Type I to 150 for Type VI)
Burn Time ≈ Base Burn Time × (6 ÷ UV Index)
Optimal exposure = 50% of burn time, max 30 minutes
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much sun do I need for vitamin D?
faq.answer: Most people need 10-30 minutes of midday sun on arms and face, 2-3 times per week. Fair-skinned people need less time; darker skin tones need more. UV index must be above 3 for meaningful production.

faq.question: Can I get vitamin D through a window?
faq.answer: No. Glass blocks UVB rays, which are needed for vitamin D synthesis. You must be outdoors or in direct, unfiltered sunlight. UVA rays (which cause aging) do pass through glass.

faq.question: Does sunscreen prevent vitamin D production?
faq.answer: SPF 30 blocks about 97% of UVB rays, significantly reducing vitamin D production. Get your brief unprotected sun exposure first, then apply sunscreen for extended time outdoors.

faq.question: Is the Sun Exposure & Vitamin D Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Sun Exposure & Vitamin D Calculator?
sections.body: The Sun Exposure & Vitamin D Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your skin type, uv index, skin exposed, you get instant results including estimated vitamin d produced, optimal exposure time, estimated time to sunburn. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting estimated vitamin d produced right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your skin type and need to find the right estimated vitamin d produced. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Skin Type (Fitzpatrick)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your skin type — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: uv index, skin exposed, minutes in sun. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your estimated vitamin d produced, optimal exposure time, estimated time to sunburn. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your skin type before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
